records.ety: [Same as Boss a stud. For senses 2 & 3 cf. D. botsen to beat, akin to E. beat.]

defs: A swelling on the skin; a large ulcerous affection; a boil; an eruptive disease.
quotes: Botches and blains must all his flesh emboss.

defs: A patch put on, or a part of a garment patched or mended in a clumsy manner.
Work done in a bungling manner; a clumsy performance; a piece of work, or a place in work, marred in the doing, or not properly finished; a bungle.
quotes: To leave no rubs nor botches in the work.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Botched (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Botching.]

defs: To mark with, or as with, botches.
To repair; to mend; esp. to patch in a clumsy or imperfect manner, as a garment; -- sometimes with up.
quotes: Sick bodies . . . to be kept and botched up for a time.

defs: To put together unsuitably or unskillfully; to express or perform in a bungling manner; to bungle; to spoil or mar, as by unskillful work.
quotes: For treason botched in rhyme will be thy bane.
